title: bridge-slack: byte-slice text truncation panics on multi-byte UTF-8
description: bridge-slack/src/bridge.rs:107: '&raw_text[..MAX_SLACK_TEXT]' slices a &str at a byte index. If a multi-byte UTF-8 char (e.g. emoji) spans the 8192-byte boundary, Rust panics with 'byte index is not a char boundary'. Any Slack message >8192 bytes containing a multi-byte char at that boundary crashes the axum handler. Axum returns 500, Slack retries indefinitely, creating an infinite crash loop. Fix: use char_indices to find the last safe boundary before MAX_SLACK_TEXT bytes.
